title: A bill to require the imposition of sanctions with respect to Ansarallah and its officials, agents, or affiliates for acts of international terrorism.
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.
plain_summary: This bill requires the President to officially label a group called Ansarallah as a terrorist organization within 30 days. Ansarallah is also known as the Houthis and is a group from Yemen that is supported by Iran. The President must also look into whether three specific people are connected to this group and report back to Congress about what they find.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1  Imposition of Sanctions with Respect to Ansarallah
sections.text: The President must take action against a group called Ansarallah within thirty days after this law is passed. The President must officially label Ansarallah as a foreign terrorist organization under existing immigration law. This means the group will face legal restrictions and penalties that apply to terrorist organizations. After making this official designation, the President has another thirty days to report to Congress about three specific people. The President must tell the Senate Foreign Relations Committee and the House Foreign Affairs Committee whether these three people work for Ansarallah or help the group in any way. The three people are Abdul Malik al-Houthi, Abd al-Khaliq Badr al-Din al-Houthi, and Abdullah Yahya al-Hakim. In this law, Ansarallah means the movement that goes by several names including Ansarallah, the Houthi movement, or any other name they might use.
